name: Nijiya Market
tagline: Boutique Japanese grocery with impeccable quality sushi, onigiri, and hard-to-find Japanese ingredients.
parking: Shared lot at Ala Moana area
talkStory: Nijiya is a California-based Japanese grocery chain founded in 1986 in San Diego, known for its quality-over-quantity approach. The Honolulu location is smaller and more curated than Marukai, but the prepared foods section has impeccable sushi, onigiri made fresh daily, and bento boxes that rival what you'd find in a Tokyo depachika. The produce section stocks pristine Japanese vegetables and the snack aisle is a treasure hunt for rare Kit-Kat flavors and seasonal mochi.
